name: ydb-recall-bench-v1
description: Curated, MIT-clean memory-QA set for measuring recall quality. Each query paraphrases the fact in its gold memory; distractors share domain/keywords so recall is non-trivial. No external/licensed data.

corpus.text: The user prefers tabs over spaces for indentation in all languages.
The user prefers minimal, single-line git commit messages without body text.
The user wants dark mode enabled by default in every editor and terminal.
The user always rebases feature branches before merging so history stays linear.
The user dislikes emoji in code comments and documentation.
The team chose PostgreSQL as the primary transactional database for the billing service.
The analytics pipeline uses ClickHouse for fast columnar aggregate queries.
Redis serves as the cache layer and ephemeral session store across services.
MongoDB was evaluated and rejected during the 2024 datastore review for lacking strong transactions.
The frontend is built with React and TypeScript using Vite as the bundler.
The backend API is written in Go and deployed as containers on Kubernetes.
Message passing between services goes through NATS rather than Kafka to keep ops light.
Alice Tan is the DevOps lead and owns the CI/CD pipeline and on-call rotation.
Bob Reyes is the staff frontend engineer responsible for the design system.
Priya Nair is the product manager for the payments squad.
Carlos Mendez leads the data platform team and owns the warehouse roadmap.
The payments squad reports to Priya, who reports to the VP of Product, Dana Lee.
The Q3 launch of the new checkout flow is scheduled for September 15, 2026.
The security audit must be completed before the checkout launch, owned by Alice.
The annual infrastructure budget for 2026 is capped at 1.2 million dollars.
The company offboarding policy requires revoking all access within 24 hours of departure.
Production deploys are frozen during the last week of December for the holiday change freeze.
The staging environment runs in the us-west-2 AWS region; production runs in us-east-1.
Secrets are stored in HashiCorp Vault and injected at deploy time, never committed to git.
Observability uses Prometheus for metrics and Grafana Loki for log aggregation.
The database backups run nightly at 02:00 UTC and are retained for 30 days.
TLS certificates are issued by Let's Encrypt and auto-renewed by cert-manager.
The decision to adopt a monorepo was made to simplify cross-service refactors.
Feature flags are managed through LaunchDarkly with a kill-switch convention for risky launches.
The mobile app is built with Flutter to share one codebase across iOS and Android.
The user's primary working hours are 9am to 5pm in the India Standard Time zone.
The user wants tests written with pytest and prefers fixtures over setUp methods.
The incident retro for the March outage concluded the root cause was an unbounded retry loop.
The new hire onboarding buddy program pairs each joiner with a senior engineer for 30 days.
Rate limiting on the public API is set to 100 requests per minute per API key.
The design system uses an 8-pixel spacing grid and a restricted 6-color palette.
The data warehouse is BigQuery, loaded hourly by a Dagster orchestration pipeline.
The customer support tool of record is Zendesk, integrated with the internal admin panel.
The user asked to always run ruff and mypy before considering a Python change complete.
Email delivery is handled by Postmark for transactional mail and a separate pool for marketing.

queries.q: what indentation style does the user want
how should I phrase git commit messages for this user

what is the user's branch merge workflow

who runs our ci cd and on-call
who owns the design system engineering
who is the product manager for payments
who leads the data platform team
who does the payments squad report up to
when is the checkout flow launching
what must finish before we launch checkout
what is the infra budget cap this year
how fast must we revoke access when someone leaves
when are deploys frozen
which aws regions host staging and production
where do we keep our secrets
what do we use for metrics and logs
when do database backups run and how long are they kept
who issues our tls certificates
why did we adopt a monorepo
how do we manage feature flags
what framework is the mobile app
what timezone and hours does the user work

what caused the march outage

what is our data warehouse and how is it loaded
what checks must pass before a python change is done
